assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets discuss mortgage rates and broader market conditions but do not provide a verified DGS10 print at or above 5.00% on any business day. No conclusive evidence to mark resolved.
No auction-result details (tail vs When-Issued at 1:00pm ET and bid-to-cover) are included in today’s news bullets, so the condition cannot be confirmed or rejected.
None of the bullets report EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report refinery utilization readings, so there is no clear evidence of a week at or above 95.0%.

While multiple items reference Iran-war-related disruptions and higher oil prices, there is no cited announcement by Maersk/MSC/CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd/ONE of a new or increased Middle East/Persian Gulf security/war-risk surcharge meeting the $500/TEU (or $1,000/FEU) threshold and effective within 30 days.
Today’s bullets include a SpaceX IPO-filing rumor but do not mention SK hynix filing any SEC registration statement for a U.S.-listed ADR or other U.S. public listing.
The Arm-related bullet is about stock movement/market narrative, not a company disclosure recognizing revenue from Arm’s own in-house CPU/processor product distinct from IP licensing/royalties.
No bullet indicates OpenAI has launched a paid, generally available text-to-video developer offering with published pricing and documentation.

News reports a jury finding Meta and YouTube negligent in a social-media addiction trial and separate Meta layoffs, but there is no evidence of a disclosed litigation settlement accrual/charge ≥$500M specifically tied to CSAM-related matters in a 10-Q/10-K/8-K.
No bullet reports a Moody’s/S&P/Fitch downgrade of a U.S.-focused private-credit fund/vehicle managed by the named firms, nor any notching/IG status change tied to deteriorating credit/NPLs.
Today’s bullets do not mention Ukrainian reporting (or Reuters/NYT citing official statements) of a 24-hour period with at least 700 Russian drone launches.
